mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the housing market index (HOUST) over the next 5 trading days signals an increase in consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over the next 60 trading days signals a decrease in supply chain capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 20 trading days signals an increase in labor market slack, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the housing market index (HOUST) and a 1-standard-deviation decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over the next 5 trading days signal an increase in consumer spending and a decrease in supply chain capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the next 20 trading days signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 0.5% rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 60 trading days signal a decrease in supply chain capacity and an increase in labor market slack, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the housing market index (HOUST) and a 1-standard-deviation decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over the next 20 trading days signal an increase in consumer spending and a decrease in supply chain capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in at least 70% of triggered instances.
